---
title: "StrictSemiring"
generated: true
description: "A semiring whose `binary_op` is associative and distributive."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.semiring`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.semiring._types`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:StrictSemiring -->
```python
class StrictSemiring(Semiring[S])
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:StrictSemiring -->
A semiring whose `binary_op` is associative and distributive.

This is the strict variant of [`Semiring`](../nitrix-semiring/Semiring.qmd): it additionally
asserts that `binary_op` is associative and distributes over
`monoid.merge`. The strict subtype unlocks reduction reorderings
that are unsafe in general: tree reductions, sub-block recombination,
cross-device reduce-scatter. Functions whose correctness depends on
those transforms annotate their argument as [`StrictSemiring`](../nitrix-semiring/StrictSemiring.qmd)
so the type-checker rejects callers passing a relaxed
[`Semiring`](../nitrix-semiring/Semiring.qmd).

The Pallas streaming kernels accept either type: they fix the
reduction order, so they do not rely on associativity. Annotating
them with [`Semiring`](../nitrix-semiring/Semiring.qmd) (rather than [`StrictSemiring`](../nitrix-semiring/StrictSemiring.qmd))
reflects this.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:StrictSemiring.as_relaxed -->
```python
as_relaxed() -> 'Semiring[S]'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Re-tag this strict semiring as a relaxed [`Semiring`](../nitrix-semiring/Semiring.qmd).

Drops the associativity/distributivity obligation, yielding a
plain [`Semiring`](../nitrix-semiring/Semiring.qmd) carrying the same fields.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:StrictSemiring.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(monoid: Monoid[S], binary_op: Semigroup, identity: Any = None, annihilator: Any = None, name: str = 'semiring', matmul_vjp: Optional[SemiringMatmulVJP] = None, ell_matmul_vjp: Optional[SemiringELLMatmulVJP] = None) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
